nodes.label: AI as writing and grammar assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI sparingly at my job. I only use it to make sure that my grammar and spelling are correct on the emails I send to clients.

nodes.label: Preservation of human writing voice
nodes.grounding_span: I want to preserve my voice as I am the sole owner and operator at my business.

nodes.label: AI for practical field strategies
nodes.grounding_span: my industry is completely in the field so there are a lot of different factors

nodes.label: AI as future defect-checking tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for nuanced inspection
nodes.grounding_span: It's possible that at some point I'll be able to use AI to double check issues I'm concerned about

nodes.label: Effective professional communication
nodes.grounding_span: make sure that my grammar and spelling are correct on the emails I send to clients

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal integrity
nodes.grounding_span: I want to preserve my voice as I am the sole owner and operator at my business.

nodes.label: Professional judgment and expertise
nodes.grounding_span: someone would still need to know what they were looking at enough to even consider it to be a problem

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for simple syntax issues
nodes.grounding_span: Grammarly catches a lot more issues than my computer does

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of personal voice
Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: It's possible that at some point I'll be able to use AI to double check issues

nodes.label: Selective adoption based on task fit
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI sparingly at my job. I only use it to make sure that my grammar and spelling are correct

nodes.label: Pragmatic workaround when tool fails
nodes.grounding_span: I transfer it to something else, like a Google Doc, use Grammarly there, and then copy it back
